description: Brave, stalwart folk as sturdy as the stone kingdoms they carve inside mountains.
benefit: Start with +2 HP. Roll hit points per level with advantage.

description: Ethereal, graceful people who revere knowledge and beauty. Elves see far and live long.
benefit: You get a +1 bonus to attack rolls with ranged weapons or a +1 bonus to spellcasting checks.

description: Green, clever beings who thrive in dark, cramped places. As fierce as they are tiny.
benefit: You can't be surprised.

description: Towering, tusked warriors who are as daring as humans and as relentless as orcs.
benefit: You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls with melee weapons.

description: Small, cheerful country folk with mischievous streaks. They enjoy life's simple pleasures.
benefit: Once per day, you can become invisible for 3 rounds.

description: Bold, adaptable, and diverse people who learn quickly and accomplish mighty deeds.
benefit: You gain one additional talent roll at 1st level.

description: Humanoid descendants of dopplegangers that retain part of their shapeshifting abilities. They hide among humans for most of their lives.
benefit: Can use innate illusion magic to change facial features (enough to be a perfect disguise). The changes remain in place until altered. Cannot use this ability again until resting.

description: Dark Elves live deep underground and rarely interact with surface dwellers. They come from a violent and barbaric society.
benefit: Highly resistant to magic spells, imposing disadvantage to spellcasting checks made against them.

description: Deep Dwarves live deep underground and rarely interact with surface creatures. They are accustomed to long trips through vast caving complexes.
benefit: Can carry an extra 3 gear slots.

description: Deep Gnomes live in the dark depths underground. They can change their skin tones to become slightly camouflaged against the rocks.
benefit: Gain advantage on all stealth checks.

description: Humanoid descendants of dragons that developed their own culture. They tend to avoid others.
benefit: Can make a breath attack on a close target (DEX check vs opponent AC, 1d6 damage) instead of taking a combat action.

description: Forest gnomes live peaceful lives in the forest among sylvan creatures and fae. They have a natural talent for illusion magic.
benefit: Can cast a minor illusion spell — a small motionless visual illusion no larger than their own body — that remains active for 5 rounds. Cannot use again until resting.

description: Gold Dwarves are cunning merchants and traders, often found in wandering caravans crossing exotic lands.
benefit: Gain a 10% discount on purchased items (including starting equipment) and a 10% bonus when selling treasure.

description: Gold Elves are the most common Elven folk. They live in towns on the edge of forests and trade extensively with other groups.
benefit: Gain a +1 bonus on ranged attacks in combat and a +1 bonus on spellcasting checks.

description: Gray Elves are arrogant and withdrawn, believing themselves to be the one true Elven ancestry.
benefit: Cannot be interrupted during rest periods.

description: Gray Orcs are the most common Orcish folk. They are aggressive border reavers and suffer from a poor reputation as a result.
benefit: Gain a +1 damage bonus with melee weapons.

description: Humanoids with large feathery wings protruding from their shoulders. They are only capable of short-range flights.
benefit: Can float about two feet off the ground and move up to near distance. Cannot use again until resting.

description: Hill Dwarves are the most common Dwarven folk. They live in large underground cities with their clan.
benefit: Gain 2 extra hit points at character creation and advantage on hit point rolls at each level up.

description: A larger and more civilized descendant of goblins. They are highly militaristic and disciplined combatants.
benefit: Can wear any armor and use any weapon, regardless of class.

description: Diminutive reptilian humanoids with a knack for survival. They can be found on the surface but tend to live underground.
benefit: Gain a +4 bonus on death timer rolls.

description: Reptilian humanoids that live in the marshes in tribal groups. They hunt by floating near the surface of the water and ambushing prey.
benefit: Can breathe underwater. Must return to the surface at least once per hour to replenish oxygen fully.

description: Mountain Dwarves live in wild mountainous regions where others fear to tread. They have frequent wars with denizens of the deep.
benefit: Immune to all poisons, including magical ones.

description: Barbarian raiders that prey upon civilized folk. They produce almost no goods of their own aside from weapons and armor.
benefit: Gain +2 AC against melee attacks in combat.

description: The most common wee folk. They are happy and charming people with a positive outlook even under grim circumstances.
benefit: Can turn invisible for 3 rounds. Cannot use again until resting.

description: The most common of Gnomish folk. Known for their keen nose and talent for mechanical work and alchemical concoctions.
benefit: Advantage on all checks to disarm traps. On a natural 1, you do not trigger a trap you are disarming.

description: Snow Elves live deep in tundra forests and often serve as guides and scouts for arctic expeditions.
benefit: Make stabilization rolls with advantage (does not extend to rolls to stabilize the Snow Elf themselves).

description: Adventurous travelers that leave home after adolescence, only returning when they have made a small fortune.
benefit: Gain a +3 bonus to initiative rolls.

description: Descendants of Gold Elves who migrated deep into the forest. They are master woodsmen.
benefit: Make bow attacks with advantage (does not extend to crossbows).

description: Feathered humanoid birds with wings, arms and a bird head with a strong beak.
benefit: You can fly, but not while wearing armour.

description: An aquatic, scaly-skinned humanoid that lives in the world's oceans with webbed hands and feet.
benefit: You breathe air and water. Your walking and swimming speed is near range.

description: A large, muscular, shaggy goblinoid brute that prefers to ambush enemies.
benefit: Advantage on Dexterity checks to sneak or hide. If you surprise your enemy, gain +1 to melee damage.

description: A hybrid equestrian with humanoid torso of fey origins.
benefit: You move twice as fast when you move without using an action.

description: An artificial humanoid-shaped construct of some kind — robot, golem, or similar.
benefit: You do not eat, breathe or drink. You are immune to disease and poison. You gain only half the benefits of magical healing.

description: Humanoid wingless, bipedal dragons that breathe energy or elemental forces.
benefit: As an action, exhale destructive energy at enemies in close range. Roll 1d20 + CON or CHA modifier, dealing 1d6 damage. Damage type is determined by your draconic ancestry (acid, cold, fire, electrical, or poison).

description: A tiny winged fey humanoid that is naturally infused with magic.
benefit: You can make seeds open and flowers blossom. You can fly, but not while wearing armour.

description: Mortals with demonic and devilish heritage, tainted by the hellish abyss.
benefit: You are immune to damage from fire.

description: A magical half-giant that lives in enchanted forests. Communicates telepathically with animals and plants.
benefit: You detect the presence of magic effects within near range.

description: Small folk with big eyes and pointed ears that live in the wilderness, forests and underground burrows.
benefit: Through sounds, gestures and simple telepathy you can communicate with natural animals that aren't monsters.

description: A broad-chested humanoid with a bull head and supernatural sense of direction.
benefit: Advantage on Wisdom checks related to navigation and tracking. Your horns make unarmed melee attacks that deal 1d4 damage.

description: An amorphous intelligent creature that changes shape with no internal organs. Communicates telepathically with any creature it can see.
benefit: You can squeeze through any narrow space or small gap.

description: A reptilian humanoid that lives in wild swamps and forests.
benefit: You have scaly tough skin that grants +1 AC if not wearing armour.

description: A green and bulky, ravenous giant that regenerates wounds — even severed limbs. Always on the hunt for tasty meat.
benefit: If you fall to zero hit points, make a DC 12 Constitution check to have 1 hit point instead. Any burning effect prevents regeneration.

description: An amphibious green humanoid reptile with a shell on its back.
benefit: You gain a +2 bonus to AC and can't wear armour.

description: A humanoid from another world.
benefit: You communicate telepathically with any creature you can see. Read the surface thoughts of a creature you can see within near range.

description: A green humanoid with waxy skin covered in spines.
benefit: Immune to poison. Direct contact with your spines is poisonous — DC 12 Constitution check or paralyzed for 1d4 rounds.

description: A humanoid with feline features.
benefit: Expert at climbing with advantage on related checks. You climb at your full speed. When you fall, you take half damage.

description: Mortals with celestial heritage blessed by the angels and heaven.
benefit: As an action, you stabilize a dying creature in close range.

description: A tree-like lifeform with bark skin and leaves. Communicates telepathically within near range.
benefit: As an action, root into the ground — while rooted, can't be moved, shoved, or knocked prone. Once per day, while in sunlight or rain, you can regain 1 hit point.

description: A captivating humanoid with iridescent scales and a beautiful voice.
benefit: As an action, one creature of LV 4 or less that can hear you within near range must make a DC 12 Charisma check or be paralyzed for 1 round.

description: A reptilian humanoid with coiling snakes for hair and scaled skin.
benefit: As an action, one creature of LV 2 or less that you look at within near range must make a DC 12 Constitution check or be petrified.

description: A humanoid with bioluminescence and the lower extremities of a jellyfish.
benefit: You always hover just off the ground. Once per day as an action, you glow brightly with heatless light illuminating out to near distance for one hour.

description: A humanoid with large, powerful legs.
benefit: You can jump near range. You have advantage on checks related to jumping.

description: A small, pointy-eared sprite with a nose for treasure.
benefit: You can detect treasure through barriers within double near range.

description: A magical humanoid made of bronze, stone, or crystal that has broken free of its creator's control.
benefit: You do not eat, breathe or drink. Hostile spellcasting checks that target you have disadvantage.

description: A supernatural lycanthrope hybrid werewolf.
benefit: You take half damage from non-magical sources.

description: A humanoid with insect features and four arms.
benefit: You have two additional arms. They cannot hold a shield, wield a weapon, or cast spells.

description: A human that can transform into an animal.
benefit: As an action, transform into one animal for one hour. Your stats and size stay the same. Animals: ape, badger, bat, bear, bird, boar, cat, fish, frog, octopus, rat, snake, or wolf.

description: A supernatural humanoid with a third telekinetic eye. Communicates telepathically with any creature within near range.
benefit: You can slowly move an object within far range weighing 1 gear slot or less.

description: A humanoid that has risen as the undead but retains their intelligence.
benefit: You do not need to eat, drink, breathe or sleep. You still need 8 hours of rest to gain its benefits.
